Home

I'm Sammy

I am a test bed engineer.

I am an aerospace robotics student at Harvey Mudd College passionate about autonomous navigation and space exploration. I enjoy designing rover test beds and experiments that evaluate autonomous navigation tasks, like identifying sources of localization error or performing image segmentation. I'm researching Mars-Analog environments and I'm designing a high-fidelity simulation tool for Mars rovers with the Unreal Engine, which addresses the sim-to-real gap: the ability of modern simulation tools to emulate realism between robotics sensor inputs and feedback.

Projects Skills Contact Me Resume

Areas of Interest

  • Flight Test Software Engineering
  • Terrain-relative Navigation
  • Vehicle Safety Engineering
  • Risk Modeling, Simulating, and Integration

Coursework

CSCI035: Computer Science for Insight

Gain experience with machine-learning and artificial-intelligence systems for file-handling, API interaction, and scripting. Ask inquiries, deploy self-designed projects, and aim to compute meaningful answers.

PHIL179E: Philosophy of Artificial Intelligence

Review historical literature about artificial intelligence (AI). Consider the differences between learning, programming, and understanding. Ask whether consciousness is emergent from human-made machines. Discuss the ethical concerns of AI and its impact on human social life.

CSCI070: Data Structures / Program Development

Learn about priority queues and dynamic dictionaries. Consider efficient data structures like heaps, self-balancing trees, and hash tables. Evaluate these in terms of worst-case scenarios, average case, and amortized analysis. Explore storage allocation and reclamation.

ENGR191: Advanced Problems in Engineering

Develop simulations for 1) autonomous navigation tasks and 2) for networks of radio-frequency (RF) Ultra-wide Band (UWB) sensors. Co-author academic work on large-language models (LLMs) for generating synthetic datasets of Lunar and Martian terrain classes.

Machine Learning and Autonomy for Diverse Domains (MADD)

Harvey Mudd College

A lab dedicated to researching and designing safe perception and localization for autonomous navigation tasks, prioritizing adaptive solutions for ground, air, and space.

Publications

  • Synthetic Martian Terrain Generation Using Adaptive Diffusion Models

    Procedia Computer Science, Volume 268, 2025, Pages 208-217

    Complex Adaptive Systems Conference with Theme: Trandiscplinary Systems and Solutions for Adaptability, CAS 2025

    March 5-7, 2025

    MIT Campus, Wong Auditorium

    Terrain classifiers rely on high-quality datasets for Lunar and Mars rovers to navigate hazardous terrain. However, there's a significant lack of real Lunar and Martian terrain imagery, and datasets like NASA's AI4Mars and the LabelMars project categorize a maximum of 6 terrain classes, excluding rare terrain edge cases. We bridge gap with an adaptive framework to generate synthetic terrain imagery, including edge cases.

Projects

ECHO - Exploration of Celestial Homes for Observation (demo)

C++ Python Entity-Component Frameworks Unreal Engine's Graphical Blueprints 3D Modeling and Lighting Linux Shell Scripting

A prototype high-fidelity 3D test bed for simulating autonomous navigation capabilities of Mars planetary rovers in a controllable Mars-Analog environment. The test bed allows users to perform experiments which evaluate the outputs of autonomous navigation tasks (e.g., rover surface terrain image segmentation).

Pilgrim (TurtleBot3) Planetary Rover

ROS 2 Foxy Python Linux (Ubuntu 20.04) TurtleBot3 Gazebo Classic RViz

Pilgrim is a ROS 2 TurtleBot lidar noise injection node in normal and dust / sandstorm conditions. I include a demo here for demonstration of TurtleBot3 navigation with ML-based LiDAR sensor noise injection and comparison.
